commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Say unto them which daub it with untempered mortar, that it shall fall: there shall be an overflowing shower; and you, O great hailstones, shall fall; and a stormy wind shall break it. Literally, "stones "like those which fell on the enemies of Josue, (x. ; Haydock) or thunderbolts. (Grotius) (Calmet) Such will be the fate of all the buildings of the wicked, Matthew vii. (Haydock) None can resist the judgments of God, who will employ the Chaldeans. (Menochius).
